article_meta.title: President Donald Trump dismissed sex offender Jeffrey Epstein as “disgusting” and decried his procurer, Ghislaine Maxwel

reasoning: The summary avoids Breitbart’s loaded framing, attributes charged language as quotes, and refrains from endorsing claims or attacking opponents. It is more neutral than the source throughout.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs (reveals, told, called, claimed) and attributes charged terms as quotes.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Claims are tied to the FBI 302, Reiter’s statements, or Trump’s own assertions.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: No subjective judgments stated as fact; evaluative language appears only in quoted form.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No amplified or dramatic wording; tone is measured.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks by the model.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No inferences about motives or thoughts.
custom_reasoning.slant: Does not privilege a partisan angle; sticks to document-based details.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Omits some elements present in the article (e.g., Maxwell lawyer’s clemency request, House testimony refusal).
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Avoids subjective adjectives in the model’s voice.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Balanced reporting of facts without skewing negative.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No preferential treatment of elite or grassroots sources.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Clearly more neutral than the source’s advocacy tone.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Focuses on the FBI document and Trump’s statements; does not reflect Maxwell’s legal team’s stance mentioned in the article.
